title: ipomopsis aggregata subsp. carmenensis
intro: Ipomopsis aggregata subsp. carmenensis, also known as Carmen's scarlet Gilia, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the phlox family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Carmen's scarlet Gilia include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and exhibit antioxidant activity. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
